steps.title: Keep verb and object together
steps.content: The phrase is "gathers to select which sites to add next." The words after the blank form the direct object of the infinitive "to select," so no punctuation should separate the verb from that object.

steps.title: Remove false punctuation
steps.content: A uses a semicolon before a dependent noun clause. B uses a colon after an incomplete construction. C inserts a comma between an infinitive and its object. The clean construction keeps "select" directly attached to "which sites to add." The answer is D.
